title: Sampling, Inference, Regression, and Causal Limits · Quantity Clinic: Confidence Intervals
summary: Evaluate a program change from sampled observational and experimental evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int confidence intervals increment in an inference and limitations brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.
Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Confidence Intervals evidence
steps.instruction: The observed values are 13, 17, 21, 17 minutes. Calculate their mean.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes minutes or min.

steps.title: Defend the Confidence Intervals model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Confidence Intervals applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
